items.preview: Hydropower remains the backbone of Laos’ power sector and the foundation of its export-driven electricity strategy. According to Laos Power Outlook, Update 2026 by GlobalData, large hydropower accounted for 72.7% of installed capacity in 2025 and 71.5% of annual generation, underlining the technology’s continued dominance.

insight2.s1: Yet the outlook to 2035 is not one of simple expansion
insight2.s2: Instead, Laos is navigating a more complex landscape: managing dry-season supply risk, responding to sovereign debt pressures, expanding cross-border trade, and accelerating wind and solar deployment
insight2.s3: Implication: stakeholder leverage appears to be rotating, which can change policy sequencing and implementation timing in energy, with $12.6bn as a measurable reference point for scenario tracking.

items.title: Bankable pathways to scale Asean power integration
items.snippet: Asean’s decarbonisation challenge is both urgent and structural. Electricity demand is set to more than double by 2050, yet fossil fuels still dominate the generation mix and progress in renewable deployment has not translated into an economy-wide emissions decline.
